title: 2026 Bolder Futures Fellowship: AI for Social Good — Fully Remote, Fully Transformative
summary: The 2026 Bolder Futures Fellowship (BFF), sponsored by Micron, is a 12‑week, fully remote professional development program designed to bridge the gap between emerging AI technologies and the social impact sector. This fellowship empowers both individuals and nonprofit organizations by strengthening career pathways, expanding operational capacity, and ensuring equitable access to practical, workplace‑ready AI skills. Applications are open until June 30, 2026 . About the Fellowship Program Duration A structured 12‑week fall program focused on equipping fellows with the tools, knowledge, and hands‑on experience needed to thrive in a rapidly evolving professional environment. Fellows will apply their learning directly to support the operational capacity of a nonprofit partner. Program Purpose The fellowship is designed to: Build practical, real‑world AI skills for professionals and emerging leaders. Strengthen nonprofit organizations by integrating AI‑driven solutions into their workflows. Provide equitable access to AI training for individuals and organizations committed to social good. Bridge the gap between theoretical AI concepts and immediate, applied use cases. Who Should Apply The program is ideal for: Professionals seeking to upskill in AI for workplace readiness. Emerging leaders interested in applying AI to social impact. Individuals committed to supporting nonprofit organizations through technology‑driven capacity building. Why Apply Participants will benefit from: Hands‑on, interactive AI training. Real‑world application of AI tools within a nonprofit setting. Professional development aligned with the future of work. A structured learning environment that moves beyond theory into practical implementation. Compensation Fellows receive: A $2,000 stipend upon successful completion of the program. Access to training valued at $7,000 or more , offered at no cost to participants. Detailed Fellowship Information Why Apply The fellowship offers a unique opportunity to: Gain workplace‑ready AI skills. Strengthen your resume with real nonprofit project experience. Contribute to meaningful social impact. Access high‑value training at no cost. Build a professional network of peers, mentors, and nonprofit leaders. Who Should Apply Ideal applicants include: Early‑career and mid‑career professionals. Individuals transitioning into AI‑related roles. People passionate about social good and nonprofit innovation. Applicants seeking remote, flexible professional development. Benefits Fellows will receive: A $2,000 completion stipend. A fully remote, flexible learning experience. Hands‑on AI training and practical application. A nonprofit partner to collaborate with on real projects. A professional portfolio demonstrating AI‑driven solutions. Access to a community of social impact innovators. Application Process Applicants should prepare: A completed online application form. A letter of commitment from a nonprofit partner (if applicable). References who can speak to the applicant’s professional or academic background. Responses to application questions demonstrating interest in AI and social impact. Frequently Asked Questions What is the Bolder Futures Fellowship: AI for Social Good? A 12‑week remote fellowship providing AI training and nonprofit capacity‑building experience. How much time is required? The program spans 12 weeks during the fall, with structured training and nonprofit project work. Is this virtual or in‑person? The fellowship is fully remote . What do fellows gain? Practical AI skills Hands‑on nonprofit project experience A professional network A $2,000 stipend Is prior AI experience required? No. The program is designed for beginners and intermediate learners. Is the fellowship paid? Yes. Fellows receive a $2,000 stipend upon completion. What is the final deliverable? A completed AI‑supported project or solution for the nonprofit partner. Do I need to be a student? No. The fellowship is open to professionals and emerging leaders. Can international students apply? Yes, as long as they meet program requirements and can participate remotely. Can a nonprofit apply directly? Nonprofits cannot apply as fellows but may partner with applicants or host fellows. Is there an age minimum? Applicants must meet the program’s minimum age requirement (typically 18+). What types of nonprofits are eligible? Nonprofits committed to social good, capacity building, and operational improvement. Can nonprofits outside the U.S. participate? Yes, nonprofit partners may be based outside the United States. Do applicants need to secure their own nonprofit partner? Applicants may bring their own partner or be matched through the program. Can a nonprofit sponsor more than one fellow? Yes, a single nonprofit may host multiple fellows. Can I apply if I’m not based in the U.S.? Yes. The fellowship is fully remote and open to global applicants. What does the application require? Application form References Nonprofit commitment letter (if applicable) Can I save my application and return later? Yes, the application platform allows saving progress. What should the nonprofit partner’s letter include? Confirmation of partnership Commitment to support the fellow Description of organizational needs Agreement to collaborate throughout the program Who should I list as references? Supervisors, mentors, professors, or colleagues who can speak to your skills and character. Who do I contact for questions? The fellowship team provides contact details on the application page. Will I be notified of my application status? Yes, all applicants receive updates regarding their status. What is the application timeline? Applications close on June 30, 2026 . What does a nonprofit partner commit to? Providing guidance and collaboration Supporting the fellow’s project work Engaging throughout the 12‑week program What do nonprofit partners gain? Increased operational capacity AI‑driven solutions tailored to their needs Access to trained fellows ready to support their mission Apply here For more information about this opportunity visit here Discover more fellowships opportunities here The post 2026 Bolder Futures Fellowship: AI for Social Good — Fully Remote, Fully Transformative appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: 19 Fully-Funded PhD Opportunities at University College Cork Under the Rinn Artificial Intelligence Programme (Starting October 1, 2026)
summary: The Research Ireland Rinn network, launched in June 2026, represents a major national investment in research excellence, innovation, and societal transformation. With a total investment of €2.85 million, the initiative aims to address the most pressing global challenges through interdisciplinary collaboration, cutting-edge research, and strong partnerships across academia, industry, government, and civil society. Rinn Artificial Intelligence serves as Ireland’s national hub for AI and data science research. Its mission is twofold: To advance foundational, world-leading research in Data Science and Artificial Intelligence. To promote open, international, interdisciplinary, and transdisciplinary research that develops strategies to address societal, cultural, economic, and environmental challenges. As part of this initiative, University College Cork (UCC) is offering 19 fully-funded PhD studentships within its Futures for Artificial Intelligence and Data Analytics programme. These positions span multiple research themes and disciplines, ensuring a diverse and inclusive cohort of future AI leaders. Programme Structure and Training Experience Alongside their individual PhD research, students will participate in a structured, cohort-based training programme designed to build advanced academic, technical, and professional skills. This includes: 1. Transferable Skills Training Communication skills Ethics and responsible research Entrepreneurship and innovation 2. Group-Based Activities Hackathons Spin-out sprints Collaborative workshops 3. Domain-Specific Training Machine learning Statistics Data management Programming Qualitative methods Digital policy methods 4. Peer-Led Learning Opportunities Research seminars Peer-designed workshops Reading groups 5. Career and Skills Development Students will receive tailored support to prepare for careers in academia, industry, and public service. Industry Engagement and Enrichment Opportunities Each PhD candidate will benefit from extensive engagement with industry and public sector partners. Enrichment experiences may include: Industry training placements Academic research visits to internationally renowned institutions Other professional development activities These experiences are designed to equip graduates with the skills and networks needed to thrive in diverse career pathways. Funding Details Each PhD scholarship is valued at €150,000 and includes: €25,000 annual stipend for four years (tax-free) Funding for conference travel, equipment, and training Full tuition coverage for EU students Fee waivers for non-EU students provided by University College Cork Deadline: Applications are reviewed fortnightly until all positions are filled. Application Review Process Applications are reviewed every two weeks. Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted for interviews. Successful candidates must later apply formally to UCC following institutional requirements. UCC strongly encourages applications from under-represented groups and is committed to equality, diversity, and inclusion. For more information about this opportunity and to apply visit here Discover more scholarship opportunities here The post 19 Fully-Funded PhD Opportunities at University College Cork Under the Rinn Artificial Intelligence Programme (Starting October 1, 2026) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Break Into Tech AI Developer Scholarship: Your Pathway Into the Future of AI Development
summary: The Break Into Tech AI Developer Scholarship—powered by Skillcrush by PowerToFly and funded by the Zero Irving Workforce Development Project Fund —is a transformative opportunity designed to open doors for aspiring AI developers from all backgrounds. With the tech industry evolving rapidly and AI reshaping every sector, this scholarship provides a rare chance for beginners and career changers to gain the skills, confidence, and support needed to enter the world of AI development. Skillcrush, the educational arm of PowerToFly, has already trained over 200,000 students across 148 countries , making it one of the most accessible and inclusive tech education platforms globally. This scholarship continues that mission by offering 15 fully supported spots for learners who want to break into tech—no prior experience required. What This Scholarship Offers The Break Into Tech AI Developer Scholarship is designed to give learners a comprehensive, beginner‑friendly pathway into AI development. Selected applicants will receive: 1. AI Development Training Participants will gain foundational and advanced skills in AI development, including: Understanding AI and machine learning concepts Working with AI tools and frameworks Building AI‑powered applications Applying AI skills to real‑world projects 2. Job Search Support Skillcrush’s career support team provides: Personalized job search guidance Resume and portfolio development Interview preparation Access to hiring networks 3. Beginner‑Friendly Learning Environment Skillcrush is known for its supportive, flexible, and inclusive learning model. This scholarship is ideal for: Complete beginners Career changers Individuals returning to the workforce Anyone passionate about AI and tech Why Skillcrush? Skillcrush has built a global reputation for making tech education accessible, practical, and empowering. As part of PowerToFly, a leading platform for diverse talent in tech, Skillcrush combines industry expertise with a mission‑driven approach. Key strengths include: A proven track record of training 200,000+ students A global community across 148 countries Courses designed for real‑world job readiness A commitment to diversity, equity, and inclusion in tech How to Apply The application process is simple and takes only 3 minutes to complete. Applicants must submit their information through the official application form to be considered. Important Details Number of spots available: 15 Application deadline: August 1, 2026 Eligibility: Beginners welcome; no prior tech experience required Why You Should Apply This scholarship is more than just a learning opportunity—it’s a launchpad into one of the fastest‑growing fields in the world. With AI transforming industries from healthcare to finance to creative arts, the demand for AI developers continues to rise. This program equips you with the skills and support needed to confidently enter the job market. Whether you re starting fresh or pivoting careers, this scholarship offers: High‑quality training Career support A supportive learning community A chance to join the future of tech Start Your Application Today If you’re ready to take the next step toward a career in AI development, now is the time. With only 15 spots available, early applications are encouraged. Apply before August 1, 2026 to be considered. title: Young NCB Advisory Group: Empowering Young Voices to Shape a Better Future for Children and Young People in the UK
summary: The Young NCB Advisory Group (YNCB) is a dynamic and inclusive platform created by the National Children’s Bureau (NCB) to ensure that children and young people have a meaningful voice in shaping the policies, programmes, and decisions that affect their lives. This advisory group brings together passionate young people who want to speak out, influence national conversations, and contribute to improving the lives of children and young people across the United Kingdom. YNCB members play a central role in NCB’s governance, research, and policy work. Their insights, lived experiences, and perspectives help guide the organisation’s projects, ensuring that young people remain at the heart of every initiative. What Is the Young NCB Advisory Group? The Young NCB Advisory Group is a youth-led body that provides advice, feedback, and direction to the National Children’s Bureau. It is designed to amplify the voices of young people and ensure they are actively involved in shaping national work related to children’s rights, wellbeing, and development. YNCB members contribute to: Governance discussions Research projects Policy development Programme design and evaluation Strategic decision-making The group serves as a safe, supportive, and empowering community where young people can express their views, share experiences, and collaborate with peers and professionals. Who Can Join YNCB? YNCB is committed to inclusivity and welcomes young people from diverse backgrounds. Membership is open to: Young people aged 11–18 Young people aged 11–24 who have a disability or special educational need No prior experience is required. The only expectation is a willingness to speak up, share ideas, and contribute to improving the lives of children and young people. What Does Being a YNCB Member Involve? Membership in the Young NCB Advisory Group offers a rich and rewarding experience. Young people participate in a variety of activities designed to build confidence, develop skills, and create real impact. Key Responsibilities and Activities Attend Six Meetings Per Year Meetings take place both online and in-person at NCB’s London offices. Engage in Governance and Policy Work Members provide insights that help shape national programmes and organisational decisions. Participate in Research and Consultations Young people contribute to studies and consultations on issues affecting children and young people. Represent NCB in Public Spaces Members may speak at events, engage with professionals, and share their perspectives with decision-makers. Support Programme Development Members help shape youth-focused projects and initiatives. Benefits of Joining the Young NCB Advisory Group YNCB offers a wide range of benefits designed to support personal growth, leadership development, and meaningful participation. No cost to participate — all travel and expenses are fully covered. Vouchers provided for taking part in activities. Opportunities to influence national work on issues affecting children and young people. Skill development , including public speaking, teamwork, leadership, and communication. Access to exclusive opportunities , such as: Leading workshops for professionals Recording podcasts Interviewing new staff Speaking with Members of Parliament A supportive and inclusive environment that accommodates accessibility, travel, and dietary needs. A chance to build friendships , join a community, and collaborate with like‑minded young people. Why YNCB Matters The Young NCB Advisory Group ensures that young people are not just consulted but are actively involved in shaping the systems and structures that impact their lives. Their voices influence national conversations, guide organisational priorities, and help create a more equitable and supportive environment for children and young people across the UK. Deadline: Monday 6 July Apply here For more information about this opportunity visit here Discover more fellowship opportunities here The post Young NCB Advisory Group: Empowering Young Voices to Shape a Better Future for Children and Young People in the UK appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: The 2027 Lipman Family Prize: A Global Celebration of Social Impact, Innovation, and Transformative Leadership
summary: Lipman Family Prize at the University of Pennsylvania stands as one of the world’s most respected recognitions for social impact organizations. With a mission rooted in vision, inspiration, and sustainable change , the prize honors organizations that demonstrate bold leadership, innovative solutions, and the ability to transfer their practices across diverse global contexts. The 2027 Lipman Family Prize application opens on July 1st , inviting social innovators from around the world to compete for one of the most prestigious awards in the social sector. About the Lipman Family Prize The Barry Marie Lipman Family Prize is an annual global award that celebrates organizations working to create a better, more equitable world. It is anchored in three core values: Vision – The ability to imagine a world transformed through bold ideas. Inspiration – The power to motivate change and mobilize communities. Impact – The measurable, sustainable outcomes that improve lives and environments. The prize is not just an award—it is a long-term partnership with the University of Pennsylvania, offering winners access to networks, learning opportunities, and global visibility. What Makes the Lipman Family Prize Unique? The prize is designed to uplift organizations that: Demonstrate innovative approaches to solving social challenges Show proven impact in their communities Have models that are transferable and scalable Exhibit strong leadership and organizational capacity Are committed to continuous learning and improvement 2026 Prize Statistics The 2026 cycle demonstrated the global reach and influence of the Lipman Family Prize: $550,000 in unrestricted cash prizes Applications from multiple countries Representation across all continents These numbers reflect the prize’s commitment to supporting diverse, high-impact organizations worldwide. 2026 Winners: A Glimpse Into Global Excellence 1. 2026 Grand Prize Winner: Kheyti Primary Areas of Impact: Economic Development, Environmental Sustainability, Poverty Alleviation Geographic Focus: Asia Kheyti empowers smallholder farmers by doubling their incomes and protecting them from climate shocks. Their flagship innovation, the Greenhouse-in-a-Box , is a low-cost, modular greenhouse paired with end-to-end support services. This model enhances climate resilience and promotes sustainable agriculture. 2. 2026 Winner: Barefoot College International Primary Areas of Impact: Economic Empowerment, Environment Climate Change, Gender Equality Geographic Focus: Global Barefoot College International trains rural women—often grandmothers—as solar engineers, educators, and entrepreneurs . Their work expands renewable energy access while advancing gender equality and economic independence in underserved communities. 3. 2026 Winner: Food for All Africa Primary Areas of Impact: Hunger, Economic Development, Environmental Sustainability Geographic Focus: Africa Food for All Africa operates West Africa’s first and largest food bank , rescuing surplus food and redistributing it to vulnerable populations. Their circular model also converts organic waste into valuable resources, reducing environmental impact while addressing food insecurity. Additional Resources Apply for the Lipman Prize Read about Lipman Fellows Explore Press Media Updates Watch Award Ceremony Videos Deadline The 2027 Lipman Family Prize application opens on July 1st . For more information about this opportunity and to apply visit here Discover more award opportunities here The post The 2027 Lipman Family Prize: A Global Celebration of Social Impact, Innovation, and Transformative Leadership appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Digital Transformation in the Public Sector: A Comprehensive Training Programme by The Hague Academy for Local Governance
summary: Digital transformation has become a defining priority for governments worldwide. As public institutions navigate rapid technological change, the need for strategic leadership, institutional readiness, and citizen‑centred innovation has never been more urgent. The Hague Academy for Local Governance addresses this global demand through its intensive, practice‑oriented course: Digital Transformation in the Public Sector , taking place from 16–20 November 2026 in The Hague, Netherlands. This in‑depth programme equips public sector professionals with the knowledge, tools, and leadership skills required to design, implement, and sustain digital reforms that enhance governance, transparency, and citizen engagement. Course Overview Governments increasingly recognise the transformative potential of digitalisation. However, true digital transformation extends far beyond adopting new technologies. It requires: Institutional capacity Policy coherence Ethical data governance Organisational change management Cross‑sector collaboration A culture of innovation This course supports professionals from government institutions, development organisations, and innovation units to understand and shape digital transformation in their own contexts. Through expert‑led sessions, peer learning, and field visits to pioneering Dutch institutions, participants explore how digitalisation can strengthen governance, improve public services, and foster citizen participation. What Participants Will Learn The programme covers a wide range of themes essential for modern public administration, including: Organisational change management Responsible integration of Artificial Intelligence Data‑driven decision‑making Public data ethics Cybersecurity resilience Governance models for digital transformation Citizen‑centred service design Institutional culture and innovation Participants engage in hands‑on exercises, real‑world case studies, and collaborative action planning to apply these concepts to their own institutions. Key Learning Objectives By the end of the course, participants will be able to: Conduct a digital maturity assessment of their organisation and identify priority areas for improvement. Integrate policy considerations for designing inclusive, accessible, and citizen‑responsive digital public services. Apply change management strategies to lead digital reform within public institutions. Use data‑driven approaches to strengthen policy development and service delivery. Analyse ethical and legal dimensions of AI and identify success factors for responsible AI adoption in the public sector. Develop a practical action plan to implement digital transformation initiatives in their home institutions. Course Structure and Experience The training programme is highly interactive and grounded in real‑world practice. Participants engage in: Self‑assessments Group discussions Peer‑to‑peer learning Collaborative exercises Field visits to Dutch institutions leading in digital governance Previous course visits included: Ministry of Infrastructure and Water Management Participants explored how the ministry enhances online citizen participation in infrastructure projects. Utrecht Data Walking Tour A hands‑on experience demonstrating how municipal data is used to improve public services. Municipality of The Hague Visits to innovation teams to understand how data improves operational efficiency and service design. Key Information Course Dates: 16–20 November 2026 Location: The Hague, Netherlands Course Fee: €2,950 (includes training materials, drinks, and warm lunches) Discounts: Available for groups of three or more Certificate: Participants receive a professional certificate from The Hague Academy Reduced Fee: Available for professionals from DAC‑listed ODA‑recipient countries Scholarship Opportunity A fully funded scholarship is available for municipal professionals: Under the age of 38 From countries on the DAC list of ODA‑recipient nations Working in local government Passionate about driving digital innovation Application Deadline: 15 July 2026 Experts Involved The course features leading experts in digital governance, including: Dr. Dara Sherwani Acting Head of Information Science, American University of Kurdistan. Researches user trust in online content and predictive social media behaviours. Shahenda ElNaggar Programme Manager at The Hague Academy, specialising in digital governance and public policy in the MENA region. Jèron Perus Strategic Advisor at the Dutch National Office for Identity Data, responsible for safeguarding citizen data. Practical Information Participants can access additional details on: Payment Visa applications Cancellation policies Course brochure download Apply here For more information about this opportunity visit here Discover more courses here The post Digital Transformation in the Public Sector: A Comprehensive Training Programme by The Hague Academy for Local Governance appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: NZ Scholarships 2026: Fully Detailed Guide to ASEAN Short Courses for Mid‑Career Professionals
summary: The Nanaaki NZ Scholarships continue to open doors for professionals across ASEAN and the Pacific by offering high‑impact, fully funded short courses designed to strengthen leadership, technical expertise, and sector‑specific capacity. The 2026 cycle features a diverse set of programmes delivered either online or in New Zealand , each tailored to address critical development priorities such as public health, trade policy, geothermal energy, disaster risk reduction, and public sector leadership. This comprehensive article provides a broad, SEO‑optimized overview of all available programmes, including delivery format, course dates, application deadlines, and detailed eligibility requirements. It is designed to help applicants understand the full scope of opportunities and prepare for a successful application. Overview of Available Programmes for 2026 The 2026 Nanaaki NZ Scholarships offer the following short courses: Public Health Management (Online) Trade Policy (In New Zealand) Public Sector Leadership – Mid Level (In New Zealand) Geothermal Energy Project Management (In New Zealand) Disaster Risk Reduction for Resilience – Online (1) Each programme targets mid‑level professionals with at least five years of relevant experience and intermediate English proficiency. 1. Public Health Management (Online) Delivery: Online Course Dates: 19 October – 20 November 2026 Applications Open: 2 August 2026 Programme Description This online course equips mid‑level public health professionals with advanced management skills to strengthen health systems, improve service delivery, and enhance public health outcomes. Participants gain exposure to global best practices, leadership strategies, and practical tools for managing public health programmes. Eligibility Requirements Citizenship from: ASEAN Secretariat, Brunei, Cambodia, Indonesia, Lao PDR, Malaysia, Myanmar, Philippines, Singapore, Thailand, or Viet Nam At least five years of relevant work experience in a public health management role Intermediate English language proficiency 2. Trade Policy (In New Zealand) Delivery: In New Zealand Course Dates: 19 October – 6 November 2026 Applications Close: 19 July 2026 Programme Description This course strengthens the capacity of trade policy professionals to analyze, negotiate, and implement trade agreements. Participants engage with New Zealand’s trade experts, explore global trade frameworks, and develop practical skills in policy formulation and international economic cooperation. Eligibility Requirements Citizenship from: Cambodia, Lao PDR, Thailand, Viet Nam, Indonesia, Philippines, ASEAN Secretariat, Timor Leste, or Malaysia At least five years of relevant work experience in the trade policy sector Intermediate English language proficiency 3. Public Sector Leadership – Mid Level (In New Zealand) Delivery: In New Zealand Course Dates: 12 October – 6 November 2026 Applications Close: 12 July 2026 Programme Description This programme focuses on enhancing leadership capabilities among mid‑level public service officials. Participants learn strategic planning, governance, ethical leadership, and public sector innovation, preparing them to lead reforms and improve public service delivery. Eligibility Requirements Citizenship from: Cambodia, Lao PDR, Thailand, Viet Nam, Indonesia, Philippines, ASEAN Secretariat, Timor Leste, Malaysia, Brunei, or Singapore At least five years of relevant work experience in the public service Intermediate English language proficiency 4. Geothermal Energy Project Management (In New Zealand) Delivery: In New Zealand Course Dates: 19 October – 17 November 2026 Applications Close: 26 July 2026 Programme Description This specialized course supports professionals in the geothermal energy sector by strengthening their project management, exploration, drilling, procurement, and strategic planning skills. Participants engage with New Zealand’s world‑leading geothermal experts and visit operational sites. Eligibility Requirements Citizenship from: Indonesia or the Philippines At least five years of relevant work experience in geothermal energy Suitable roles include: exploration manager, drilling manager, procurement manager, construction manager, project manager, or strategic planning roles Intermediate English language proficiency 5. Disaster Risk Reduction for Resilience – Online (1) Delivery: Online Course Dates: 21 September – 23 October 2026 Applications Close: 12 July 2026 Programme Description This online course strengthens the capacity of disaster risk reduction (DRR) practitioners to build resilience, reduce vulnerabilities, and improve preparedness. Participants explore risk assessment, early warning systems, community resilience, and climate‑related disaster management. Eligibility Requirements At least five years of relevant work experience in disaster risk reduction Intermediate English language proficiency Why These Programmes Matter These short courses are designed to: Strengthen regional capacity in critical development sectors Support mid‑career professionals in advancing their leadership and technical skills Foster collaboration between New Zealand and ASEAN member states Promote sustainable development, resilience, and innovation across the region How to Apply Applicants must submit their applications through the official Nanaaki NZ Scholarships portal once applications open. Each programme has its own deadline, so applicants should prepare early and ensure all documents meet the requirements. Apply here For more information about this opportunity visit here Discover more scholarship opportunities here The post NZ Scholarships 2026: Fully Detailed Guide to ASEAN Short Courses for Mid‑Career Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: 3rd Call for Proposals for ReCIPE Big Research Grants: Up to £100,000 for Research on Conflict, Economic Growth, and Public Policy
summary: The Reducing Conflict and Improving Performance in the Economy (ReCIPE) programme has officially launched its third call for proposals for Big Research Grants , offering substantial funding to support innovative, policy-relevant research at the intersection of conflict, economic growth, and public policy. This competitive funding scheme is coordinated under the Growth Research Platform (GRP) , a CEPR initiative funded by the UK Foreign and Commonwealth Office (FCDO). This call is designed for researchers and multidisciplinary teams seeking to generate impactful evidence that can shape policy in ReCIPE’s focus countries. With grants of up to £100,000 , this funding opportunity supports ambitious research projects that require significant resources, data, and field engagement. Deadline: 11 August 2026, 23:59 BST About the ReCIPE Big Research Grants The Big Research Grants (BRGs) aim to advance high-quality research that deepens global understanding of how conflict interacts with economic growth and public policy. These grants are intended for projects that exceed the scope of ReCIPE’s Compact Research Grants (CRGs), requiring more extensive data collection, fieldwork, or research assistance. Applicants must submit proposals using the official templates available on the ReCIPE website and upload them through the CEPR Hub portal . Detailed submission instructions are provided on the How to Apply to ReCIPE webpage. Funding Amount and Eligible Costs Each Big Research Grant provides up to £100,000 to support: Research assistance Data collection and/or data purchase Researcher stipends Travel to field sites (including when using secondary data) In-country activities (with strong justification and safety considerations) All budgets must comply with the ReCIPE BRG Budget Guidelines , and proposals must demonstrate cost effectiveness and value for money . Research Themes Proposed research must align with one or more of ReCIPE’s core themes: Public policies for peace Private and public investments and peace Institutions, democracy, and peace Geoeconomics Climate change, natural resources, and conflict Information and conflict (media, public opinion, big data, forecasting) Ethnic diversity and nation-building Peacemaking, peacebuilding, and reconstruction Gender, inequality, and conflict Projects addressing cross-cutting issues will receive special consideration, including: New data generation Natural resources and climate change Gender equality and inclusion Policy Relevance Requirement A key evaluation criterion is the relevance of the research to policy in ReCIPE Focus Countries . Projects outside these countries must clearly justify how their findings will inform policy in at least one focus country. Multidisciplinary Collaboration Encouraged Given the complexity of ReCIPE’s themes, the programme strongly encourages multidisciplinary teams , combining expertise from economics, political science, sociology, data science, environmental studies, and related fields. Eligibility and PhD Student Participation PhD students are eligible to apply. However, due to the scale and complexity of BRGs, applicants will be assessed on their experience with large-scale research. PhD students are encouraged to apply with senior co-investigators to strengthen feasibility and competitiveness. Why Apply for a ReCIPE Big Research Grant? This funding call is ideal for researchers seeking to: Conduct large-scale or multi-country studies Generate new datasets Explore innovative methodologies Influence policy in conflict-affected or fragile contexts Collaborate across disciplines How to Apply Applicants must: Use the official proposal templates Submit via the CEPR Hub portal Follow all instructions on the How to Apply to ReCIPE webpage Ensure budgets comply with BRG guidelines Demonstrate policy relevance and methodological rigor Conclusion The 3rd Call for ReCIPE Big Research Grants represents a major opportunity for researchers committed to understanding and addressing the complex relationships between conflict, economic growth, and public policy. With generous funding and a strong emphasis on policy impact, this call supports transformative research that can shape development outcomes in some of the world’s most challenging environments. Deadline Reminder: 11 August 2026, 23:59 BST Apply here For more information about this opportunity visit here Discover more grant opportunities here The post 3rd Call for Proposals for ReCIPE Big Research Grants: Up to £100,000 for Research on Conflict, Economic Growth, and Public Policy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Spirit of Togetherness Award 2026: Empowering Young Leaders Building Connection & Belonging
summary: In a world rapidly transformed by digitalisation, mobility, and shifting social norms, the need for genuine human connection has never been more urgent. Communities everywhere are experiencing both new opportunities for interaction and rising challenges such as isolation, fragmentation, and loss of belonging. Against this backdrop, young leaders are stepping forward to reimagine how people gather, share, and build meaningful relationships. The Spirit of Togetherness Award 2026 , presented by Pernod Ricard in partnership with One Young World , celebrates these changemakers. Building on the success of the WE Award 2025, this initiative recognises young innovators who are creating inclusive spaces, fostering cultural exchange, and strengthening social cohesion through the power of conviviality. About the Spirit of Togetherness Award Pernod Ricard defines conviviality as the powerful act of bringing people together—whether through shared meals, cultural traditions, conversations, or everyday moments that spark joy, trust, and understanding. This award honours young leaders who embody this spirit by designing initiatives that promote belonging and connection. As communities evolve, young adults are increasingly becoming architects of inclusive environments—both physical and digital—where diversity is celebrated and every voice is valued. Their work demonstrates that connection is not accidental; it is intentionally created, nurtured, and sustained. The Spirit of Togetherness Award 2026 recognises five outstanding young leaders whose initiatives are transforming how people come together. What the Award Offers Each of the five selected award recipients will receive: €8,000 grant to expand and scale their initiative Access to a global network of Conviviality Ambassadors Opportunities for international visibility, collaboration, and impact amplification This award is designed not only to support individual leaders but also to inspire a global movement centred on empathy, shared experiences, and human connection. Focus Areas for Eligible Initiatives Candidates must be leading an initiative that aligns with one or more of the following themes: Facilitating joyful, shared moments within communities, workplaces, or cultural spaces. Creating inclusive environments that bridge diverse groups—such as intergenerational, multicultural, or differently‑abled communities. Crafting everyday moments of cultural togetherness , rooted in local heritage, traditions, food, drink, and cultural expression. These initiatives should demonstrate how conviviality can strengthen social cohesion, foster inclusion, and enrich cultural life. Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: Must be at least 18 years old and meet the legal drinking age in their country of residence Must be 35 years old or younger Open to nationals of all countries Must demonstrate: Impact – measurable community engagement and positive outcomes Inclusivity – commitment to bringing diverse people together Scalability – potential for replication and growth Additional consideration may be given to initiatives that incorporate environmental sustainability or innovation . Application Details Opening Date: 8 June 2026 Application Deadline: 20 July 2026 How to Apply: Visit the official One Young World application portal Number of Awardees: 5 This award is a unique opportunity for young leaders to elevate their work, gain global recognition, and join a network of changemakers committed to building a more connected world. Conclusion The Spirit of Togetherness Award 2026 champions young leaders who are redefining how communities connect. By spotlighting their work, Pernod Ricard and One Young World aim to inspire a global shift toward more inclusive, empathetic, and convivial societies. The future of community-building lies in shared experiences—and this award empowers the leaders shaping that future. Apply here For more information about this opportunity visit here Discover more award opportunities here The post Spirit of Togetherness Award 2026: Empowering Young Leaders Building Connection Belonging appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: TechCongress Congressional Fellowships: Comprehensive Guide to the January 2027 Application Cycle
summary: TechCongress has officially opened applications for its January 2027 Congressional Fellowship cohort , offering a unique and highly impactful pathway for technologists to serve directly in the U.S. Congress. This prestigious program places scientists, engineers, cybersecurity experts, data specialists, and other technology professionals into Congressional offices to advise lawmakers on some of the most pressing technology and policy issues of our time. Application Deadline: June 28, 2026 at 11:59 PM ET. This article provides a detailed, expansive overview of the fellowship, including eligibility, application expectations, program structure, professional development opportunities, and the overall experience of serving on Capitol Hill as a TechCongress Fellow. About TechCongress and Its Fellowship Programs TechCongress is an independent 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization dedicated to bridging the gap between technology and policymaking. Through its fellowship programs, TechCongress places highly skilled technologists into Congressional offices to serve as trusted advisors on issues such as: Artificial intelligence Cybersecurity Data privacy Emerging technologies Digital infrastructure National security Technology regulation Internet governance TechCongress operates two primary fellowship tracks: Senior Congressional Innovation Fellowship – Designed for mid‑career professionals with extensive experience. Congressional Innovation Fellowship – Designed for early‑career technologists seeking to enter the policy space. Both tracks provide fellows with the opportunity to work directly with Members of Congress or Congressional Committees, contributing technical expertise to legislative processes, hearings, briefings, and policy development. Fellowship Eligibility and Career Tiers TechCongress uses a structured tier system to determine eligibility and stipend levels. Applicants must calculate their total years of professional experience after completing an undergraduate degree. Early‑Career Track Requires 2–6 years of total professional experience. Includes years spent in graduate education (Master’s or PhD). Includes full‑time military service for U.S. Veterans. Mid‑Career Track Requires 8 or more years of total professional experience. Intermediate Tier Applicants with 7 years of experience fall into an intermediate category. These applicants may select both tiers during application, allowing TechCongress to determine final placement and stipend level. Applicants must provide their undergraduate graduation month and year to verify eligibility. Application Process for the January 2027 Fellowship Applications are now open and will close on June 28, 2026 at 11:59 PM ET . The application process includes: Completing the online application form Submitting required essays (an offline version is available for download) Providing professional background information Selecting the appropriate fellowship tier Optional nomination of colleagues or peers who may be a strong fit TechCongress also offers a nomination portal for individuals who wish to recommend someone for the fellowship. Events, Webinars, and Application Resources Throughout the recruitment cycle, TechCongress hosts multiple events to support applicants. These sessions provide: Guidance on completing the application Insights from former fellows Tips on crafting strong essays Information about the fellowship experience Live Q A with the TechCongress team Recordings of past events are available on: The TechCongress blog The TechCongress YouTube channel The TechCongress videos page Upcoming events are listed on the TechCongress events page. Preparing Fellows for Congress TechCongress provides extensive preparation to ensure fellows are ready to navigate the complexities of Capitol Hill. This includes: Orientation sessions Policy training Legislative process education Workshops on communication, writing, and stakeholder engagement Ethics and conflict‑of‑interest guidance Mentorship from former fellows and policy experts Fellows are equipped with the knowledge and tools needed to contribute meaningfully from day one. Fellowship Experience: Working on Capitol Hill Fellows serve as policy advisors to Members of Congress or Congressional Committees. Their responsibilities may include: Drafting legislation Preparing policy memos Conducting research Advising on technology‑related issues Supporting hearings and briefings Engaging with stakeholders Providing technical expertise during negotiations The day‑to‑day experience varies depending on the office placement, but fellows consistently report high levels of responsibility, impact, and professional growth. Professional Development and Mentorship TechCongress invests heavily in the long‑term success of its fellows. Professional development opportunities include: One‑on‑one mentorship Career coaching Networking with policymakers, technologists, and industry leaders Access to a growing alumni network Skill‑building workshops Leadership development sessions These resources help fellows transition into impactful roles in government, civil society, academia, and the private sector. Post‑Fellowship Opportunities and Impact TechCongress fellows have gone on to influential positions such as: Senior advisors in federal agencies Technology policy leaders in nonprofits Directors of cybersecurity and AI governance Academic researchers Technology consultants Legislative staffers Public interest technologists The fellowship serves as a powerful launchpad for careers at the intersection of technology and public policy. Conflicts of Interest and Ethical Standards TechCongress maintains strict ethical guidelines to ensure fellows serve with integrity. Applicants must disclose potential conflicts of interest, including: Financial interests Employment relationships Industry affiliations TechCongress provides guidance on how to manage or mitigate conflicts to maintain transparency and trust. How to Apply Applicants can access the application portal through the TechCongress website. Additional resources include: Offline essay download Nomination form FAQ page Fellowship overview pages Events and informational sessions Deadline Reminder: Applications close on June 28, 2026 at 11:59 PM ET .
